bids.reasoning: I am targeting maximum utility at the opening because time pressure is nonexistent at t=0.00, and the opponent’s first offer gives me the lowest value on every issue, so it shows no useful concession pattern yet. I will not react to the deadlock threat as binding and will counter with a strong valid-domain bid.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a small concession rather than repeating my exact prior all-valueA offer. The opponent has held firm at all valueE and used threats of deadlock, but their behavior shows no real concession yet, so I will only concede on my lower-weight issues D and E.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is little need to make large concessions yet. The opponent has only slightly conceded from all valueE to issueA:valueD while using threats, so I treat the messages as pressure tactics and concede mainly on my lower-weight issues D and E while holding firm on the more important A, B, and C.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.88 because it is still very early (t=0.06), so there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low-value offers or react strongly to threats. The opponent has made only a small concession on issueA while holding the rest at valueE, so I will concede further only on my lower-weight issues while protecting issueA, issueB, and issueC.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while continuing a measured concession path rather than reacting to threats. The opponent has made only small concessions and their messages are aggressive cheap talk, so I will hold firm on the highest-weight issues A and B while conceding further on low-weight issues D and E to signal movement.

I am targeting a high utility bid of about 0.80 because it is still early at t=0.10, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers yet. The opponent has made only small concessions, with their latest bid improving issueA but keeping most issues at valueE, while their threats are cheap talk rather than binding. I have already conceded on the lowest-weight issues D and E, and now make a modest concession on issueC while holding firm on the highest-weight issues A and B.

I targeted a still-strong utility around 0.76, conceding further on issueC while holding firm on the two highest-weight issues, issueA and issueB. Time is still early at t=0.12, so I should not move close to the opponent’s low-utility offers yet; their messages are threatening, but their actual offers show only limited movement toward my preferences.

I am targeting a still-high utility bid around 0.74 because it is early in the negotiation and the opponent’s offers have only risen to about 0.32 utility for me despite their threats. The opponent has shown limited movement on issueA and issueB while keeping issueC-E low, so I am conceding modestly on issueB and keeping the already-conceded low-weight issues D/E at valueE, while protecting issueA.

I am targeting a utility around 0.72, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting the highest-weight issues. The opponent has made some numeric movement, especially on issueA, but their threats about deadlock are cheap talk this early at t=0.16. I am therefore holding issueA at valueA, conceding on issueB to a value they have previously signaled, and giving them the low-weight issueD/issueE positions.

I am targeting a utility around 0.70, continuing gradual concessions without giving up the high-weight issues too quickly this early at t=0.18. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly and their threatening messages are cheap talk, but their movement suggests they care about pushing values away from valueA, so I concede further on the lower-weight/medium issueC while holding issueA and issueB relatively strong.

I am targeting a utility around 0.64: still well above reservation and the opponent’s current best offer to me, but with a meaningful concession from my prior bid. At t=0.20 there is still time, so I will not accept their threats at face value, but their offers show some movement on issueA and issueB; I’ll match their latest issueA concession while holding firmer on the higher-weight issueB and issueC.

I targeted about 0.60 utility for myself, a modest concession from my prior 0.64 bid while it is still early enough not to collapse toward the opponent’s low-utility offers. The opponent’s offers have improved only slightly for me and their messages are mostly threats, so I am conceding on lower/mid-weight issueC while holding issueA and issueB at reasonable values.

I am targeting about 0.60 utility, maintaining value on the high-weight issues while offering concessions on lower-weight and contested issues. At t=0.24 there is still time, so I should not collapse to the opponent’s low-utility bids; their threats are cheap talk, though their offers show some movement especially on issueA and issueB. I am changing the package from my last bid by accepting their latest issueB/issueC direction while holding issueA, which they have previously offered as valueA.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.56, conceding further on lower/mid-weight issues while preserving strong value on issueA and some value on issueB. At t=0.26 there is still time, but the opponent’s latest offers show real movement on issueA and occasional movement on issueB/issueC, while their messages are mostly threats that I should not treat as binding. This bid avoids repeating my last offer and moves closer to patterns they have signaled, especially on issueC through issueE.

I targeted a moderate utility around 0.54: still well above reservation, but more concessive than my earlier high-utility anchors. At t=0.28 there is some time pressure but it is not late enough to accept the opponent’s very low current offer; their threats are cheap talk, while their actual offers show occasional willingness to give issueA=valueA and some movement on issueC/issueD. I am therefore holding issueA, conceding substantially on issueB/issueC, and leaving issueD/issueE at their preferred-looking low values.

I am targeting about 0.50 utility: a meaningful concession from my prior position while still preserving value on the higher-weight issues A and B. At t=0.30 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s low-utility latest offer; their messages are mostly threats, while their bids show they may tolerate movement on issueA or issueB but strongly prefer low values on issueC/D/E.
